description: Create the Phase 0 baseline document shell for the current Windows Electron Dynamic Island behavior.
steps: Create docs/mac-island-phase0-baseline.md with top-level headings for Scope, Runtime Environment, Evidence Index, and Phase 0 Acceptance.
Add a one-sentence scope note that the document records current Windows behavior before mac migration implementation.
Check that every heading is unique and the file opens correctly from repository root.

description: Create the state specification document shell for review, todo, and visual state behavior.
steps: Create docs/mac-island-state-spec.md with headings for Startup/Auth, Review Rules, Todo Rules, Visual State Machine, and Reminder Trigger Rules.
Add an empty table template under each heading with columns: Condition, Trigger, Expected UI, Evidence.
Check markdown structure consistency and confirm heading order matches the Phase 0 scope.

description: Create the interaction specification document shell for hover, click-through, pointer, and gesture behavior.
steps: Create docs/mac-island-interaction-spec.md with headings for Hover Activation, Click-Through Toggle, Pointer Gestures, and Trackpad Gestures.
Add a reusable behavior row template with fields for trigger threshold, debounce, and recovery behavior.
Check that the document contains no behavior claims yet and only shell content.

description: Create the animation specification document shell for transition timing and motion sequencing.
steps: Create docs/mac-island-animation-spec.md with headings for Expand/Collapse, Mode Switch, Hover Motion, and Reminder-Triggered Motion.
Add an animation table template with fields for duration, easing, trigger, and observed result.
Check that all headings and table columns are present and readable in markdown preview.

description: Create the music takeover specification document shell for playback-driven island behavior.
steps: Create docs/mac-island-music-spec.md with headings for Providers/Inputs, Mode Switching, Playback States, and Degraded Fallback.
Add a behavior matrix template with fields for media event, widget state change, and user-visible output.
Check that the shell is documentation-only and contains no unverified runtime conclusions.

description: Create the Phase 0 migration checklist shell used to gate the move into Phase 1.
steps: Create docs/mac-island-migration-checklist.md with sections for State, Interaction, Animation, Reminder, and Music coverage.
Add checklist rows with fields: Item, Expected Behavior, Evidence Link, Status.
Check that checklist status defaults are unset and ready for later fill tasks.

description: Add startup and authentication behavior details to the state spec from current widget code and visible runtime behavior.
steps: Trace startup and authentication-related logic in front-end/src/components/DynamicIslandWidget.tsx and front-end/electron/main.cjs.
Use browser MCP to run the visible startup path and capture one authenticated and one unauthenticated observation.
Document startup/auth condition-to-UI mapping in docs/mac-island-state-spec.md under Startup/Auth.
Check that each documented rule includes at least one concrete code or runtime evidence reference.

description: Add Review Rules rows from static code paths without runtime sampling.
steps: Trace review-related data and rendering branches in front-end/src/components/DynamicIslandWidget.tsx.
Fill Review Rules in docs/mac-island-state-spec.md with condition, trigger, expected UI, and code evidence columns.
Check that placeholder rows are removed and every new row includes at least one concrete code location.

description: Add Todo Rules endpoint and rendering rows from existing frontend and API code.
steps: Trace todo-related request and rendering paths in DynamicIslandWidget.tsx and related API call sites.
Fill Todo Rules in docs/mac-island-state-spec.md for /todos/stats, /todos/tasks, and /todos/tasks/:id/status with expected UI impact.
Check that each row contains endpoint, trigger, expected UI, and a direct code evidence reference.

description: Add Todo status-update persistence assumptions from code contracts and local schema references.
steps: Trace todo status update call flow in front-end code and backend controller/service path where available.
Add a concise contract note in docs/mac-island-state-spec.md describing write intent and expected persisted state effect.
Check the note references concrete code paths and avoids unverified runtime claims.

description: Add the Visual State Machine transition matrix from component state logic.
steps: Trace collapsed, activity, and expanded transition triggers in DynamicIslandWidget.tsx.
Fill Visual State Machine rows in docs/mac-island-state-spec.md with source condition, trigger, expected state, and evidence.
Check that one explicit row states reminder auto-open is a trigger path instead of a standalone mode.

description: Add Reminder Trigger Rules from reminderTime and guard refs in widget code.
steps: Trace reminder-related refs, key guards, and auto-open conditions in DynamicIslandWidget.tsx.
Fill Reminder Trigger Rules in docs/mac-island-state-spec.md with due condition, trigger, recovery expectation, and evidence.
Check that the section distinguishes time trigger logic from display mode definitions.

description: Add Hover Activation and Click-Through Toggle rules from renderer and Electron bridge code.
steps: Trace hover handling and ignore mouse event toggling in DynamicIslandWidget.tsx and front-end/electron/main.cjs.
Fill Hover Activation and Click-Through Toggle sections in docs/mac-island-interaction-spec.md.
Check each row includes trigger threshold or guard, recovery behavior, and evidence link.

description: Add Pointer and Trackpad gesture rules from gesture accumulators and cooldown logic.
steps: Trace gesture delta accumulators, lock flags, and cooldown timers in DynamicIslandWidget.tsx.
Fill Pointer Gestures and Trackpad Gestures sections in docs/mac-island-interaction-spec.md with action mapping.
Check that all template rows are replaced and each rule includes code evidence.

description: Add Expand/Collapse and Mode Switch animation rows from current UI animation definitions.
steps: Trace expand/collapse and mode-switch animation configuration in DynamicIslandWidget.tsx and related style hooks.
Fill corresponding sections in docs/mac-island-animation-spec.md with duration/easing/trigger/observed-result fields.
Check rows contain concrete code references and no placeholder entries remain in those two sections.

description: Add Hover Motion and Reminder-Triggered Motion animation rows from existing implementation.
steps: Trace hover-related motion and reminder-triggered open/collapse animation paths in DynamicIslandWidget.tsx.
Fill Hover Motion and Reminder-Triggered Motion sections in docs/mac-island-animation-spec.md.
Check that each row links to a specific code location and avoids inferred timing without source evidence.

description: Add music behavior rows from MusicService, MusicWorker, and renderer IPC listeners.
steps: Trace music event payload flow across front-end/electron/MusicService.cjs, MusicWorker.cjs, and DynamicIslandWidget.tsx listeners.
Fill Providers/Inputs, Mode Switching, Playback States, and Degraded Fallback in docs/mac-island-music-spec.md.
Check each row includes event trigger, state effect, user-visible result, and code evidence.

description: Populate the migration checklist with concrete evidence links from all Phase 0 spec documents.
steps: Replace template rows in docs/mac-island-migration-checklist.md with concrete checklist items for state, interaction, animation, reminder, and music.
Link each checklist item to at least one specific section row in the spec documents.
Check that every checklist item has a clear pass condition and no empty status fields for prepared rows.

description: Add a Phase 1 entry gate note after checking Phase 0 checklist coverage.
steps: Compare completed checklist categories with Phase 0 outputs and focus points in 灵动岛迁移方案.md.
Write a concise go/no-go note in docs/mac-island-phase0-baseline.md under Phase 0 Acceptance.
Check that the gate note lists explicit completion criteria and references checklist sections.

description: Create the mac-island project skeleton directory and Xcode project container for a standalone macOS app.
steps: Create mac-island/ with MemoryFlowIsland.xcodeproj and MemoryFlowIsland target group structure.
Create initial folder layout: App, Window, MenuBar, Preferences, UI, Resources under mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/.
Check that project and group paths are consistent with the Phase 1 structure plan in 灵动岛迁移方案.md.

description: Add app bootstrap entry files for the native app startup path.
steps: Add MemoryFlowIslandApp.swift, AppDelegate.swift, and SceneCoordinator.swift under mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/App/.
Wire app launch flow so SceneCoordinator can initialize menu bar and window controllers.
Check that bootstrap files compile in target membership and have no unresolved symbols.

description: Create native island window shell classes with transparent floating window behavior.
steps: Add IslandPanel.swift and IslandWindowController.swift under mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/Window/.
Configure a transparent, borderless, always-on-top style suitable for island shell behavior.
Check that window shell classes can instantiate and expose show/hide entry methods without business logic coupling.

description: Add notch-position helper placeholders for Phase 1 geometry readiness.
steps: Add NotchLayoutEngine.swift and DisplayObserver.swift under mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/Window/.
Implement minimal API surfaces for screen bounds input and top-center placement output.
Check that IslandWindowController can reference the helper APIs without compile errors.

description: Add SwiftUI island placeholder view and connect it to the native window host.
steps: Create UI/IslandRootView.swift with a simple placeholder island appearance and static text marker.
Embed IslandRootView in the native window content pipeline from IslandWindowController.
Check that app launch can render the placeholder island content inside the native window shell.

description: Add menu bar controller and base menu entries for the native app shell.
steps: Create StatusBarController.swift and StatusMenuBuilder.swift under mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/MenuBar/.
Add base menu entries: Show/Hide Island, Preferences, and Quit with stub action handlers.
Check that menu bar icon appears and menu actions are callable without app crash.

description: Add empty preferences window skeleton and wire menu action entry.
steps: Create PreferencesWindowController.swift and PreferencesView.swift under mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/Preferences/.
Wire the Preferences menu item to open the preferences window controller.
Check that preferences window can be opened repeatedly without duplicate controller lifecycle issues.

description: Add app resource placeholders and basic mac app metadata for standalone launch.
steps: Create Resources/Info.plist and Assets.xcassets placeholders under mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/Resources/.
Set minimal bundle metadata required for app launch and menu bar presentation.
Check that project build settings point to the new resource files correctly.

description: Add a short Phase 1 scaffold handoff note describing generated native app modules.
steps: Create or update a Phase 1 note under docs/ describing App, Window, MenuBar, Preferences, UI, and Resources modules.
Document explicit non-goals for this phase: no review/todo feature migration, no auth/API integration, no music provider migration.
Check that file paths in the handoff note match actual project skeleton paths.

description: Append Phase 1 acceptance-gate checklist items for native shell readiness.
steps: Add checklist items for: app can launch, menu bar icon is visible, and a basic top island window can be shown.
Place the checklist in an existing migration tracking doc used by this repository.
Check that each checklist item has a clear pass condition and is scoped only to Phase 1 skeleton goals.

description: Expose reusable screen metrics for native island positioning.
steps: Add a Window-layer screen metrics model that carries frame, visible frame, safe-area insets, backing scale, and a stable display identity.
Update DisplayObserver to return the screen metrics model instead of only a raw frame.
Check IslandWindowController can request the metrics model without parsing NSScreen directly.

description: Detect whether the target display needs notch-aware placement.
steps: Add a helper in the Window layer that marks a screen as notch-bearing or flat-top from the collected metrics.
Keep the decision isolated from IslandWindowController so later phases can reuse the same display classification.
Check both notch and non-notch sample inputs compile through the placement path.

description: Center the island shell against the notch-safe top region on notch displays.
steps: Update NotchLayoutEngine to compute the island origin from the top safe region instead of raw visible-frame midpoint placement.
Keep Phase 2 top-margin constants explicit inside the layout engine.
Check the engine returns a centered frame for a notch-display metrics fixture.

description: Place the island shell in a stable top-center fallback on non-notch displays.
steps: Add a separate fallback calculation for flat-top displays that keeps the entire shell inside the visible area.
Route the non-notch path through the same layout result type used by notch displays.
Check the fallback frame stays fully visible for a flat-top metrics fixture.

description: Show the island on the correct display using the new placement result.
steps: Update IslandWindowController show-path logic to request screen metrics and apply the layout-engine result before ordering the panel front.
Store the last applied display identity and frame in the controller for later re-anchor work.
Check repeated show calls reuse the new placement path without duplicate setup.

description: Keep the island on the same display when screen parameters change.
steps: Extend the Window/display layer to resolve the preferred screen by the last applied display identity when that screen still exists.
Fall back to the current main screen only when the previous target screen disappears.
Check a screen reorder or display attach-or-detach event can still produce one valid target display.

description: Re-anchor the island after display arrangement or resolution changes.
steps: Extend DisplayObserver callback wiring so IslandWindowController receives a single reposition signal for screen-parameter changes.
Reapply the latest layout result when the display configuration changes.
Check the observer path still compiles and triggers one reposition entry point.

description: Re-anchor the island after the Mac wakes from sleep.
steps: Register workspace wake notifications in DisplayObserver and map them to the same reposition callback used by display changes.
Keep observer cleanup symmetrical for app shutdown and controller deinit.
Check wake observation can be started and stopped without leaked observers.

description: Keep the island panel floating above normal windows without stealing focus.
steps: Refine IslandPanel style, collection behavior, and activation flags for a nonactivating, all-spaces, fullscreen-safe shell.
Keep the panel compatible with later hover interaction work.
Check showing the island does not require making the app the main active window.

description: Add shell size presets for collapsed and expanded placeholder frames.
steps: Define explicit shell size constants or presets in the Window layer for compact and expanded placeholder states.
Expose a controller path that can switch between the presets without introducing Phase 3 business state.
Check the window frame changes compile cleanly and remain centered after a preset switch.

description: Prevent the expanded shell shadow from being cropped by the panel frame.
steps: Adjust panel content hosting, padding, or shadow margins so the larger shell can render without clipping rounded edges or shadow.
Keep the visual fix inside the native shell layer rather than future business views.
Check the expanded preset keeps its visible bounds and shadow inside the panel frame.

description: Expose a native click-through toggle for the island panel.
steps: Add a controller or panel API that switches ignoresMouseEvents on and off without recreating the window.
Keep the API callable from later hover coordination code.
Check the toggle can flip in both directions while the panel remains visible.

description: Add pointer-entry detection that works while the island is in click-through mode.
steps: Introduce a small hover monitor in the native shell layer that can detect pointer entry into the island hotspot even when the panel is not accepting mouse events.
Send hover-start callbacks into IslandWindowController instead of mutating UI state directly.
Check the hover monitor lifecycle starts and stops with the window controller.

description: Switch the island into interactive hover mode when the pointer enters the hotspot.
steps: Wire hover-start events to disable click-through and keep the panel interactive while the pointer is over the island.
Keep the hover activation path isolated from later Phase 6 gesture logic.
Check entering the hotspot leads to the interactive window path without reopening the panel.

description: Return the island to click-through mode after hover exit.
steps: Add hover-exit handling and a small recovery rule that re-enables click-through after the pointer leaves the island.
Keep the recovery path in IslandWindowController so future gesture code can share it.
Check repeated enter-and-exit cycles do not leave the panel stuck in interactive mode.

description: Append Phase 2 acceptance items for window positioning and recovery behavior.
steps: Add checklist items for notch placement, non-notch fallback, display attach-or-detach recovery, resolution-change recovery, wake recovery, click-through, and hover activation in the existing migration tracking doc.
Keep each checklist item scoped to one observable window behavior with a clear pass condition.
Check the new checklist items cover only Phase 2 shell behavior and not later business-state work.

description: Add a short Phase 2 window-system handoff note for the next implementation slice.
steps: Create or update a Phase 2 note under docs/ that names the active Window-layer files and the expected acceptance path.
Record explicit non-goals: no review-or-todo data migration, no auth flow, no music takeover, and no Phase 6 gesture polish in this slice.
Check the note points only to files and behaviors that belong to the Phase 2 shell.

description: Create the Phase 3 visual token map shell for the native geometry migration.
steps: Create docs/mac-island-visual-token-map.md with headings for Compact Shell Tokens, Activity Shell Tokens, Expanded Shell Tokens, Hover and Shadow Rules, Path Sources, Seam Rules, and Evidence.
Add a one-sentence scope note that this document only covers visual geometry migration and excludes auth, todo data, review data, and music provider integration.
Check that the markdown headings are unique and the file opens correctly from repository root.

description: Record the Windows compact and activity shell token values needed for the native preview shells.
steps: Trace the compact width branches, `height = 36`, `ACTIVITY_COLLAPSED_WIDTH = 240`, `COLLAPSED_RADIUS_DEFAULT = 22`, `COLLAPSED_RADIUS_ACTIVITY = 50`, `SQUIRCLE_SMOOTHNESS = 3.5`, and `SQUIRCLE_SMOOTHNESS_ACTIVITY = 2.3` in front-end/src/components/DynamicIslandWidget.tsx.
Add rows in docs/mac-island-visual-token-map.md that map each compact or activity token to its source constant or branch and expected Phase 3 preview usage.
Check that every new row includes a direct code reference and no placeholder text remains in those two sections.

description: Record the Windows expanded, hover, and shadow token values needed for the native geometry engine.
steps: Trace `expandedWidth = 460`, `expandedMusicHeight = 210`, `expandedAppHeight = 320`, expanded radius `48`, hover scale `1.06`, and the hover or expanded shadow rule in front-end/src/components/DynamicIslandWidget.tsx.
Add rows in docs/mac-island-visual-token-map.md for expanded shells, hover behavior, shadow timing, and shadow visibility gates.
Check that the document clearly marks which tokens are preview-only in Phase 3 and which ones are deferred to later business-state work.

description: Record the Windows path-function sources and seam constraints that the native path factory must preserve.
steps: Trace `generateSquirclePath`, `generateOpenSquirclePath`, `generateLeftCapPath`, `generateRightCapPath`, and `generateEarPath` in front-end/src/components/DynamicIslandWidget.tsx.
Add rows in docs/mac-island-visual-token-map.md that map each path function to its inputs, output role, and seam-sensitive notes such as the 1px ear overlap behavior.
Check that the document explicitly states Capsule replacement is not acceptable for these shapes and that each row links to a concrete source location.

description: Create the Phase 3 geometry acceptance document shell for preview, path, and pixel verification.
steps: Create docs/mac-island-phase3-geometry-acceptance.md with headings for Preview Matrix, Path Parity, Pixel Edge Checks, External Display Scaling, Non-Goals, and Evidence.
Add an empty checklist table under each heading with columns for Condition, Expected Result, Evidence, and Status.
Check that the document contains only shell content and no unverified implementation claims.

description: Fill the Phase 3 acceptance document with the five preview shells and explicit non-goal boundaries.
steps: Add Preview Matrix rows in docs/mac-island-phase3-geometry-acceptance.md for compact collapsed, hover compact, activity compact, expanded music shell, and expanded app shell.
Add Non-Goals rows that explicitly exclude auth sync, review data, todo data, real music provider integration, and Phase 5 state-machine work from this phase.
Check that each acceptance row names an observable preview result and links back to docs/mac-island-visual-token-map.md or 灵动岛迁移方案.md.

description: Add Phase 3 geometry checklist rows to the migration checklist so later phases can gate on visual evidence.
steps: Append a Phase 3 section to docs/mac-island-migration-checklist.md for path parity, preview shell coverage, compact-state black-edge checks, ear seam checks, and external-display scaling.
Link each new checklist row to docs/mac-island-phase3-geometry-acceptance.md and docs/mac-island-visual-token-map.md.
Check that each checklist row has a clear pass condition and that status fields are prepared but not marked complete.

description: Create the native visual-geometry module scaffolding under mac-island for Phase 3 work.
steps: Create a `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/` folder with placeholder files for `IslandVisualTokens.swift`, `IslandVisualState.swift`, `IslandShapeMetrics.swift`, `IslandPathFactory.swift`, `IslandShapeEngine.swift`, and `IslandVisualStatePreview.swift`.
Add the new files to the Xcode project structure without deleting or rewriting any existing Phase 2 window-layer files.
Check that the project references compile with empty type shells and no unresolved file-path references.

description: Add a single Swift token source for compact, activity, expanded, hover, and shadow geometry values.
steps: Implement `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandVisualTokens.swift` with grouped token definitions for compact, activity, expanded music, expanded app, hover, and shadow behaviors.
Map each Swift token to the corresponding Windows source constant or documented branch from docs/mac-island-visual-token-map.md.
Check that token names are stable, compile cleanly, and do not depend on auth, reminder, todo, or music-provider state.

description: Add preview-only visual state cases so Phase 3 can render shell variants without business data.
steps: Implement `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandVisualState.swift` with preview-only cases for compactCollapsed, hoverCollapsed, activityCollapsed, expandedMusic, and expandedApp.
Expose small computed helpers that answer whether a state is expanded, whether shadow is allowed, and which token set should drive the geometry.
Check that the file has no API, auth, reminder scheduler, or music provider dependency.

description: Add shape metrics that turn a preview state plus scale into concrete geometry inputs.
steps: Implement `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandShapeMetrics.swift` so it resolves width, height, radius, smoothness, ear tension, ear blend height, scale, stroke visibility, and shadow visibility from `IslandVisualState` and a scalar input.
Keep the metrics API independent from business-state enums by accepting only preview-state and scale inputs.
Check that the resolved metrics for the five preview states match the values recorded in docs/mac-island-visual-token-map.md.

description: Port the Windows squircle body path into the native path factory for compact and expanded shells.
steps: Implement the Swift equivalent of `generateSquirclePath` inside `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandPathFactory.swift` using `Path` or `CGPath` output.
Keep the same width, height, radius, and smoothness inputs used by the Windows baseline so compact and expanded body paths can be generated from shared metrics.
Check the generated body path with at least one compact and one expanded preview input and confirm the top edge stays flat while the lower corners remain continuous.

description: Port the Windows open-stroke path into the native path factory for the states that allow an inner stroke.
steps: Implement the Swift equivalent of `generateOpenSquirclePath` inside `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandPathFactory.swift`.
Keep the path inputs aligned with `IslandShapeMetrics` so the stroke path follows the same shell geometry as the filled body path.
Check that the open-stroke path does not introduce a top-edge line and can be disabled entirely for compact states that must remain fully black.

description: Port the Windows left-cap path so the native shell can keep the compact and expanded left edge profile.
steps: Implement the Swift equivalent of `generateLeftCapPath` inside `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandPathFactory.swift`.
Drive the left-cap path from `IslandShapeMetrics` height, radius, and smoothness values instead of hard-coded preview-specific numbers.
Check that the rendered left cap touches the body path without clipping or leaving a transparent seam in both compact and expanded preview shells.

description: Port the Windows right-cap path so the native shell can keep the compact and expanded right edge profile.
steps: Implement the Swift equivalent of `generateRightCapPath` inside `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandPathFactory.swift`.
Drive the right-cap path from the same metrics model used by the left cap so both sides stay symmetric unless the source path says otherwise.
Check that the rendered right cap stays seam-free against the body path in both compact and expanded preview shells.

description: Port the Windows ear path so the native shell keeps the liquid connection and overlap behavior.
steps: Implement the Swift equivalent of `generateEarPath` inside `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandPathFactory.swift` for left and right ear generation.
Preserve the 1px overlap semantics documented in the Windows source so the ears do not visually detach from the body.
Check that both mirrored ears can render from idle, activity, and expanded metrics without exposing a transparent gap at the join.

description: Compose a single shape engine that returns every Phase 3 shell path from shared metrics.
steps: Implement `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandShapeEngine.swift` so one call can resolve body, optional stroke, left cap, right cap, and ear paths for a given `IslandShapeMetrics` input.
Keep the engine isolated from window placement and business-state logic by accepting only geometry inputs and returning path outputs.
Check that `IslandVisualStatePreview.swift` and `IslandRootView.swift` can consume the engine without duplicating any path math.

description: Render the compact collapsed preview shell with native body, cap, and ear geometry.
steps: Implement the compact-collapsed branch in `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandVisualStatePreview.swift` using `IslandShapeEngine` output.
Render the shell as pure black geometry with no debug text, no placeholder text, and no unsupported stroke.
Check the native preview window and confirm the compact collapsed shell is visible and uses the documented compact metrics.

description: Render the hover compact preview shell with the Windows hover scale and shadow behavior.
steps: Extend `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandVisualStatePreview.swift` so the hover-collapsed branch uses the same compact geometry plus the `1.06` hover scale.
Apply the Windows hover shadow rule and keep the body fill black while the hover shell is active.
Check the native preview window and confirm hover mode differs only by allowed hover geometry effects rather than by business content.

description: Render the activity compact preview shell with the Windows activity width, radius, and ear profile.
steps: Extend `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandVisualStatePreview.swift` so the activity-collapsed branch uses `ACTIVITY_COLLAPSED_WIDTH`, activity smoothness, activity radius, and activity ear metrics.
Keep the preview shell geometry-only with no review cards, todo rows, reminder content, or real activity data.
Check the native preview window and confirm the activity compact shell is visually distinct from the normal compact shell by geometry alone.

description: Render the expanded music preview shell with the Windows expanded music geometry.
steps: Extend `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandVisualStatePreview.swift` so the expanded-music branch uses width `460`, height `210`, expanded radius, and expanded ear metrics.
Allow only the stroke and shadow behaviors that are documented for expanded shells in the Windows baseline.
Check the native preview window and confirm the expanded music shell can render without any music provider dependency.

description: Render the expanded app preview shell with the Windows expanded app geometry.
steps: Extend `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandVisualStatePreview.swift` so the expanded-app branch uses width `460`, height `320`, expanded radius, and expanded ear metrics.
Keep the expanded app preview geometry-only and avoid pulling in review or todo content components during Phase 3.
Check the native preview window and confirm the expanded app shell can render independently from the expanded music shell.

description: Replace the black probe root view with a preview host that can switch between all five Phase 3 shell variants.
steps: Update `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/IslandRootView.swift` so it hosts `IslandVisualStatePreview` instead of a static black rectangle.
Add a local preview-state switch mechanism that can move between compact collapsed, hover compact, activity compact, expanded music, and expanded app without touching business data layers.
Check the native app window and confirm each preview state can be selected repeatedly without breaking the Phase 2 top attachment.

description: Apply Phase 2 top-band scaling so the visual geometry engine sizes shells correctly on notch and external displays.
steps: Add a Phase 3 scale input based on the Phase 2 top-band metrics, using the migration-plan guidance for `visualScale` rather than hard-coding a single desktop size.
Feed that scale into `IslandShapeMetrics` so compact, activity, and expanded preview shells scale from one geometry pipeline.
Check that the preview shell still attaches to the active top band and that external-display scaling does not exceed the available top width.

description: Update the native panel sizing path so preview shells request their visible size from geometry metrics instead of placeholder presets.
steps: Extend `IslandPanel.swift` and `IslandWindowController.swift` so the visible shell size can come from resolved Phase 3 geometry metrics rather than only from the old placeholder presets.
Keep the top attachment, display recovery, and click-through logic inside the existing Phase 2 window layer while the size source changes.
Check that switching between the five preview shells updates panel size without pushing the window away from the top anchor.

description: Restrict stroke and shadow rendering to the Windows-defined states so compact shells remain fully black.
steps: Centralize the stroke-visibility and shadow-visibility gates in the Phase 3 visual layer instead of scattering them across multiple preview branches.
Ensure compact collapsed and activity compact states render with no unsupported border, no white edge, and no debug decoration, while hover and expanded states follow the documented shadow rules.
Check the native preview window and confirm compact shells remain fully black and expanded or hover shells are the only ones that show the allowed effects.

description: Compare sampled native geometry output against the Windows path baseline and record any intentional differences.
steps: Generate sampled path output for the native body, open-stroke, left-cap, right-cap, and ear paths using the same width, height, radius, smoothness, tension, and blend-height inputs as the Windows functions.
Compare the native samples against a JS baseline generated from front-end/src/components/DynamicIslandWidget.tsx for compact, activity, and expanded inputs.
Record the parity result and any intentional deltas in docs/mac-island-phase3-geometry-acceptance.md with evidence links.

description: Capture the five Phase 3 preview shells and verify the edge-pixel requirements from the migration plan.
steps: Capture PNG screenshots for compact collapsed, hover compact, activity compact, expanded music, and expanded app from the native preview host.
Check each screenshot for a continuous black top edge, no non-black border in compact states, and no transparent seam where the ears meet the body.
Attach the screenshot evidence to docs/mac-island-phase3-geometry-acceptance.md and update the linked Phase 3 checklist rows.

description: Verify external-display scaling and preview-state switching after Phase 3 geometry is attached to the Phase 2 window shell.
steps: Run the native preview host on an internal notch display and an external non-notch display and switch through all five preview shells on each screen.
Confirm the preview shell stays attached to the active top band, scales from the Phase 2 metrics, and keeps shadow visibility limited to the documented hover and expanded states.
Update docs/mac-island-phase3-geometry-acceptance.md and docs/mac-island-migration-checklist.md with the observed screen-matrix evidence.

description: Add a short Phase 3 geometry handoff note so the next queue slice can start Phase 4 window sizing cleanly.
steps: Create docs/mac-island-phase3-geometry-handoff.md with sections for Implemented Modules, Accepted Preview States, Open Risks, and Explicit Phase 4 Inputs.
Summarize which Visual files became the source of truth for tokens, metrics, and paths, and link the summary back to docs/mac-island-phase3-geometry-acceptance.md.
Check that the handoff note explicitly says business data, auth, and state-machine migration remain out of scope for this completed slice.

description: Create the Phase 4 sizing and motion acceptance document shell.
steps: Create docs/mac-island-phase4-sizing-motion-acceptance.md with headings for Sizing Outputs, Content-Driven Width, Shadow Buffering, Motion Profiles, Interruptible Transitions, Preview Evidence, and Non-Goals.
Add a one-sentence scope note that Phase 4 covers native sizing and motion infrastructure only, excluding real data providers and production music integration.
Check that every heading is unique and the document opens correctly from repository root.

description: Add Phase 4 acceptance rows for sizing outputs and top attachment behavior.
steps: Add acceptance rows for visibleFrame, shadowFrame, contentFrame, and hitTestFrame outputs in docs/mac-island-phase4-sizing-motion-acceptance.md.
Add acceptance rows for notch display, flat-top display, external display, and resolution-change sizing behavior.
Link each row to the Phase 4 section in 灵动岛迁移方案.md and to the expected native modules.

description: Add Phase 4 acceptance rows for content-driven width and shadow buffering.
steps: Add acceptance rows for activity width derived from content demand, padding, notch/base width, token fallback, and display maximums.
Add acceptance rows for expanded shadow buffering that prevents bottom or side shadow clipping.
Check that the rows explicitly reject fixed activity width as the primary sizing strategy.

description: Add Phase 4 acceptance rows for Alcove-like motion quality.
steps: Add acceptance rows for compact-to-activity, activity-to-expanded, expanded-to-collapsed, hover enter, and hover leave motion paths.
Add acceptance rows for spring-like elasticity, content fade or blur timing, and interruptible transition behavior.
Check that the rows require preview evidence instead of only code inspection.

description: Add native sizing result types for Phase 4 window layout.
steps: Create a small sizing model file under mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/Window/ for visibleFrame, shadowFrame, contentFrame, hitTestFrame, and diagnostics fields.
Keep the model independent from SwiftUI view code and business data providers.
Check the full native Swift source set with swiftc typecheck.

description: Create the native IslandWindowSizingEngine shell.
steps: Create IslandWindowSizingEngine under mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/Window/ with an API that accepts visual state, top attachment metrics, and width constraints.
Return a sizing result object without moving any window or mutating controller state.
Check that the new engine compiles with the existing native source set.

description: Move snapshot-based visible size resolution into IslandWindowSizingEngine.
steps: Make IslandWindowSizingEngine call IslandShapeEngine.snapshot for the requested visual state and scale inputs.
Map the snapshot visibleFrame and contentFrame into the Phase 4 sizing result model.
Check that compact, activity, and expanded preview states still produce non-empty visible sizes in a lightweight Swift harness or typecheck-safe probe.

description: Add content-driven width constraints to the sizing engine path.
steps: Pass IslandWidthConstraints through IslandWindowSizingEngine instead of computing width only in IslandWindowController.
Keep activity width derived from content demand plus padding while retaining token fallback width.
Check that compact states with no content requirement preserve the existing compact width behavior.

description: Add display maximum clamping to Phase 4 sizing outputs.
steps: Clamp the sizing result so visible and shadow frames remain within the current display horizontal bounds where possible.
Keep the top attachment center anchored when clamping happens near screen edges.
Check a synthetic narrow-screen input to confirm the island does not extend past the display frame.

description: Wire IslandWindowController to consume IslandWindowSizingEngine results.
steps: Replace inline sizing assembly in IslandWindowController with a call to IslandWindowSizingEngine.
Keep IslandWindowController responsible only for applying panel layout, root view inputs, and placement.
Check that preview state cycling still repositions the panel from the sizing result.

description: Add sizing diagnostics for Phase 4 preview states.
steps: Add concise debug descriptions or log values for state, visual scale, horizontal scale, visible size, shadow size, content size, and hit frame.
Gate diagnostics so they can be used during preview validation without spamming production paths.
Check one compact, one activity, and one expanded sizing result includes all diagnostic fields.

description: Create synthetic sizing matrix coverage for Phase 4 displays.
steps: Add or extend a lightweight Swift harness that evaluates compact, activity, expanded music, and expanded app states on notch and flat-top screen metrics.
Write the matrix output under docs/evidence/mac-island-phase4/ as JSON.
Check that every matrix row includes visible, shadow, content, and hit frame values.

description: Tune expanded shadow buffer sizing to avoid clipped shadow edges.
steps: Move expanded shadow buffer values into a clear Phase 4 sizing or visual token path if they are still hard-coded in shape composition.
Ensure expanded bottom shadow has enough transparent buffer to fade naturally without a visible cutoff.
Check hover and compact states do not inherit expanded shadow buffer values.

description: Add motion token types for Phase 4 shell transitions.
steps: Create a motion token model under mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/ or a nearby Motion module for spring, duration, keyframe, and fade settings.
Include the Windows baseline spring values, activity open duration, activity collapse duration, segmented times, and shadow fade duration.
Check that the model has no dependency on business data or AppKit window classes.

description: Create a transition kind model for shell motion paths.
steps: Add transition kind cases for compactToActivity, activityToCompact, activityToExpanded, expandedToActivity, expandedToCompact, hoverEnter, and hoverLeave.
Expose helpers that select the correct motion tokens for each transition kind.
Check that unknown or same-state transitions fall back to a stable no-op or default profile.

description: Create the native IslandMotionEngine shell.
steps: Create IslandMotionEngine with an API that accepts previous visual state, next visual state, and transition context.
Return a motion plan for shell frame, shape path morph, shadow, and content visibility without mutating UI state.
Check that the new engine compiles and can produce plans for compact-to-activity and activity-to-expanded transitions.

description: Wire preview state changes through IslandMotionEngine.
steps: Update preview state cycling so it derives a transition kind and motion plan before updating the root view inputs.
Remove fixed ad-hoc animation durations from IslandWindowController where the motion plan should decide timing.
Check that tapping through preview states still advances compact, hover, activity, expanded music, and expanded app in order.

description: Apply motion profiles to SwiftUI shell shadow transitions.
steps: Route shadow opacity, radius, and offset animation settings through the motion token or motion plan path.
Keep hover and expanded shadow behaviors distinct and avoid expanded shadow clipping.
Check that shadow changes still compile and no compact or activity state shows unsupported shadow.

description: Add preview content visibility timing hooks.
steps: Add a small content visibility input model for opacity, blur, and delay values used by preview content layers.
Connect the model to motion profiles without adding real business content.
Check that content visibility defaults do not change pure shell rendering when no content is present.

description: Add interruptible transition state storage for preview shell motion.
steps: Add minimal state needed to remember current and target preview visual states during an animation.
Ensure a new tap or hover event can replace the target state without leaving stale lock flags.
Check rapid preview cycling does not skip root view updates or leave the panel at an old size.

description: Add Phase 4 preview controls for core motion paths.
steps: Expose a local preview-only way to trigger compact-to-activity, activity-to-expanded, expanded-to-compact, hover enter, and hover leave transitions.
Keep the controls out of business data and provider code paths.
Check each trigger updates the visual state and requests sizing from the Phase 4 sizing engine.

description: Capture Phase 4 sizing evidence for preview states.
steps: Generate docs/evidence/mac-island-phase4/sizing-matrix.json from the synthetic sizing harness.
Confirm the matrix includes notch, flat-top, compact, activity, expanded music, and expanded app cases.
Check that no row reports a clipped or negative frame.

description: Capture Phase 4 shadow evidence for expanded states.
steps: Generate or update preview captures for expanded music and expanded app shadow behavior under docs/evidence/mac-island-phase4/.
Check that bottom and side shadows fade without visible hard clipping in the captured evidence.
Record any environment limitation if capture is synthetic rather than physical-device evidence.

description: Fill the Phase 4 acceptance document with sizing evidence links.
steps: Add links to sizing-matrix.json and any preview captures in docs/mac-island-phase4-sizing-motion-acceptance.md.
Mark sizing rows as Passed only when the corresponding evidence exists.
Leave motion rows Prepared if visual motion evidence is not yet captured.

description: Fill the Phase 4 acceptance document with motion evidence links.
steps: Add links to motion preview captures, videos, GIFs, or frame-sequence evidence in docs/mac-island-phase4-sizing-motion-acceptance.md.
Mark motion rows as Passed only when compact, activity, expanded, hover, and interrupt paths have evidence.
Check that the document clearly notes any remaining real-device calibration gaps.

description: Update the migration checklist with Phase 4 sizing and motion gates.
steps: Add Phase 4 checklist rows for sizing engine outputs, content-driven activity width, shadow buffering, motion profiles, and interruptible transitions.
Link each row to docs/mac-island-phase4-sizing-motion-acceptance.md.
Check that the checklist distinguishes Prepared, Passed, and real-device-pending statuses.

description: Refresh the Phase 4 handoff notes after sizing and motion infrastructure lands.
steps: Update agent-state.md with the implemented Phase 4 modules, first remaining pending task, and recommended startup files.
Add a concise codex-progress.md record for the Phase 4 sizing and motion infrastructure outcome.
Check that the handoff does not require reading codex-progress-archive.md for normal startup.
